title: Avoid reluctant quantifiers followed by empty matches
description: Reluctant quantifiers in regular expressions should be followed by expressions that cannot match the empty string. Allowing empty matches can lead to inefficient or incorrect pattern matching, making code harder to maintain and debug.
recommendation: Update regular expressions so that any reluctant quantifier is followed by an expression that cannot match the empty string.

noncompliant.code:
Pattern.compile("a*?") // 'a*?' can match the empty string
Pattern.compile("(foo)*?bar") // '(foo)*?' can match the empty string before 'bar'

compliant.code:
Pattern.compile("a+?b") // '+?' ensures at least one 'a' before 'b'
Pattern.compile("(foo)+?bar") // '(foo)+?' ensures at least one 'foo' before 'bar'

checks.message: Update regular expressions so that any reluctant quantifier is followed by an expression that cannot match the empty string.
